subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Personal Protective Equipment
description: A fast breakdown of Personal Protective Equipment for builders.

hook.text: A surgical mask blocks 80% of outgoing droplets. An N95 blocks 95% of incoming airborne particles.

buildup.text: PPE creates physical barriers between the wearer and pathogens in the environment.

discovery.text: Gloves, gowns, masks, and eye protection each guard a different entry point for infection.

twist.text: Improper removal of PPE is when most contamination occurs—the outside is covered in pathogens.

climax.text: The correct doffing sequence—gloves, gown, mask last—prevents self-contamination.

punchline.text: Putting PPE on protects you. Taking it off wrong infects you. Order matters.

quiz.question: When is the greatest risk of self-contamination with PPE?
options: During removal, when the contaminated outside contacts skin
